definition: A reflexive feedback mechanism: larger deficits → more issuance → higher term premium → higher debt service → larger deficits. U.S. net interest expense has risen from ~$350B (FY2020) to ~$1T (FY2025/26). The loop activates when the blended borrowing rate exceeds nominal GDP growth. The UK gilt crisis (2022) validates the mechanism for a DM sovereign.
evidence: Net interest from $350B to $1T in 5 years
Blended rate (~4.5%) approaching nominal GDP growth (~5.5%)
Average debt maturity ~6.2 years → ~16% reprices annually
UK gilt crisis 2022: activated at ~100% debt/GDP with ~2pp fiscal expansion
Indirect bidder share declining from 72% to 65%; T-bill share rising from 15% to 22%
open_questions: What is the U.S.-specific activation threshold given reserve currency premium?
Can Treasury maturity management (shifting to bills) defer activation indefinitely?
Would a term premium shock trigger basis trade unwind → systemic cascade?
